service_requested: Advanced imaging (illustrative reuse of policy engine — knee OA pattern)
demo_headline: Alternate evidence pattern: naproxen PRN + daily walks are both spoken and documented.

criteria.label: Symptom duration ≥ 6 weeks
criteria.summary: Known osteoarthritis with winter-predominant pattern — chronic course documented.

evidence.quoted_span: Her known osteoarthritis of the knee is currently asymptomatic (pain 0/10 today); she reports a winter-predominant pattern managed with over-the-counter naproxen

criteria.label: Failed / incomplete conservative care ≥ 6 weeks
criteria.summary: Daily market walks documented and spoken.

evidence.quoted_span: She walks to the market daily (~30 minutes round trip).
I walk to the market and back, fifteen minutes each way, no problem.

criteria.label: NSAID / analgesic trial
criteria.summary: OTC naproxen PRN with food documented.

evidence.quoted_span: managed with over-the-counter naproxen as needed with food, without dyspepsia, melena, or knee swelling
When it acts up I take the naproxen, the little blue ones from the pharmacy

criteria.label: Red flags assessed / neurologic exam
criteria.summary: Systemic red flags reviewed for annual exam; knee-specific neurologic deficit language not the visit focus.

evidence.quoted_span: She denies chest pain, dyspnea, palpitations, dizziness, headaches, visual changes, and focal numbness or weakness.

artifact.title: One targeted question
artifact.body: Any locking, giving-way, or progressive neurologic symptoms in the affected limb that would change imaging urgency?

criteria.label: Functional limitation
criteria.summary: Today asymptomatic; winter functional impact not quantified for payer language.

fhir_gap_callout.title: Policy-engine reuse
fhir_gap_callout.detail: Same provenance checklist over a different musculoskeletal visit — shows the mechanic is criteria-JSON-swappable.

packet_draft.title: Draft evidence packet — musculoskeletal imaging criteria
